item.title: 【Building an Image Classification Pipeline With Apache Camel and Deep Java Library (DJL)】使用 Apache Camel 和 Deep Java Library (DJL) 构建图像分类管道
item.description:
【

Image classification is now a key part of many applications. Whether you’re automating photo organization, filtering uploaded content, or enriching product catalogs with visual tags, knowing what’s in an image can be just as important as knowing what a user typed.

For Java developers, the challenge is familiar: most computer vision examples live in Python notebooks, while the systems that actually need image classification run on the JVM. Bridging that gap usually means standing up a separate Python microservice, managing REST calls, and dealing with serialization overhead. That’s a lot of ceremony for what should be a single processing step.

】

图像分类现在是许多应用程序的关键部分。无论您是要自动组织照片、过滤上传的内容，还是使用视觉标签丰富产品目录，了解图像中的内容与了解用户输入的内容一样重要。

对于 Java 开发人员来说，面临的挑战是熟悉的：大多数计算机视觉示例都存在于 Python 笔记本中，而实际需要图像分类的系统则在 JVM 上运行。弥合这一差距通常意味着建立一个单独的 Python 微服务、管理 REST 调用以及处理序列化开销。对于单个处理步骤来说，这是一个繁重的仪式。

item.title: 【Why Pass/Fail CI Pipelines Are Insufficient for Enterprise Release Decisions】为什么通过/失败 CI 管道不足以做出企业发布决策
item.description:
【

Modern CI/CD pipelines have made software delivery faster and more predictable. Automated validation, repeatable builds, and rapid feedback give teams confidence that changes behave as expected. For small applications or tightly scoped systems, a simple pass‑or‑fail signal from a pipeline is often enough.

In large enterprise environments, however, that simplicity becomes a weakness.

】

现代 CI/CD 管道使软件交付更快、更可预测。自动验证、可重复构建和快速反馈让团队确信更改会按预期进行。对于小型应用程序或范围狭窄的系统，来自管道的简单通过或失败信号通常就足够了。

然而，在大型企业环境中，这种简单性就成为了弱点。

item.title: 【The Hidden Bottlenecks That Break Microservices in Production】破坏生产中微服务的隐藏瓶颈
item.description:
【

Most microservice systems don’t fail because they lack scalability. They fail because they were never designed to behave correctly under high load and stress.

A very common pattern for applications built using a micro-services architecture is this: everything runs quite normally for a long time. The architecture looks clean, services appear healthy, CI/CD tests are green, and monitoring dashboards do not raise alarms.

】

大多数微服务系统不会因为缺乏可扩展性而失败。它们失败是因为它们从未被设计为在高负载和压力下正确运行。

使用微服务架构构建的应用程序的一个非常常见的模式是：一切都运行很长时间。架构看起来很干净，服务看起来很健康，CI/CD 测试是绿色的，监控仪表板不会发出警报。

item.title: 【Observability in Spring Boot 4】Spring Boot 4 中的可观察性
item.description:
【

In microservices, you’ve likely broken a cold sweat more than once when a request suddenly 'vanishes' the moment it hits a Database or a Message Broker. It is a true operational nightmare. However, with the release of Spring Boot 4 in early 2026, building a comprehensive Observability system has become easier than ever, thanks to the 'all-in' support from micrometer tracing.

The Problem: "Anonymous" Queries

When your database starts lagging (slow queries), you check the processlist in MySQL only to find a vague line:

】

在微服务中，当请求在到达数据库或消息代理时突然“消失”时，您可能不止一次地惊出一身冷汗。这是一场真正的运营噩梦。然而，随着 2026 年初发布 Spring Boot 4，得益于 micrometer 的“全面”支持，构建全面的可观测性系统变得比以往更容易追踪。

问题：“匿名”查询

当您的数据库开始滞后（查询缓慢）时，您检查 MySQL 中的 processlist 却发现一条模糊的线：

item.title: 【The Agent Protocol Stack: MCP vs. A2A vs. AG-UI】代理协议栈：MCP、A2A 与 AG-UI
item.description:
【

If you're building AI agents in 2026, you've probably bumped into at least one of these acronyms: MCP, A2A, AG-UI. Maybe all three. And if you're anything like me, your first reaction was: "Are these competing standards? Do I need all of them? Which one do I actually use?"

Here's the short answer: They're not competing — they're complementary. Each one solves a different problem at a different layer of the agent architecture. Think of them like TCP, HTTP, and HTML — different protocols at different layers that work together to make the web function.

】

如果您在 2026 年构建 AI 代理，您可能至少遇到过以下缩写词之一：MCP、A2A、AG-UI。也许三个都可以。如果您和我一样，您的第一反应是：“这些标准是相互竞争的吗？我需要所有这些标准吗？我实际上使用哪一个？”

简短的回答是：它们不是竞争 - 它们是互补的。每个解决方案都在代理架构的不同层解决不同的问题。可以将它们想象为 TCP、HTTP 和 HTML — 不同层的不同协议，它们协同工作以使 Web 发挥作用。

item.title: 【Context-Aware Authorization for AI Agents】AI 代理的上下文感知授权
item.description:
【

In an enterprise AI system, we use already established role-based access control as a reference to perform actions.  In theory, and to an extent, that should be enough. The rule is simple: if an employee or a user has permission to a document, the system allows it; otherwise, the access is blocked. 

The problem with this simple rule is with modern AI agents – they do not behave like a traditional application. An AI agent takes a simple request, interprets it, pulls information from multiple systems, and the agent is allowed to perform actions on the user’s behalf. During these access grants and actions, the original intent is abused and adds exploited privilege to the agent that leads to reveal of information that a user (agent) should never have received in that context. 

】

在企业人工智能系统中，我们使用已经建立的基于角色的访问控制作为执行操作的参考。  从理论上讲，在某种程度上，这应该足够了。规则很简单：如果员工或用户对文档有权限，系统就会允许；否则，访问将被阻止。 

这个简单规则的问题在于现代AI 代理 - 它们的行为与传统应用程序不同。人工智能代理接受一个简单的请求，对其进行解释，从多个系统中提取信息，并且允许代理代表用户执行操作。在这些访问授权和操作期间，原始意图被滥用，并向代理添加了被利用的特权，从而导致用户（代理）在该上下文中永远不应该接收到的信息被泄露。

item.title: 【Can Claude Skills Replace Playwright Agents? A Practical View for QA Engineers】克劳德技能能否取代剧作家经纪人？质量保证工程师的实用观点
item.description:
【

AI in test automation is exploding and everyone is trying Claude Skills with Playwright to make QA engineering magic happen.

One question I often hear is:

】

测试自动化中的人工智能正在爆炸式增长，每个人都在尝试Claude Skills with Playwright来实现QA工程奇迹。

我经常听到的一个问题是：

item.title: 【Vercel AI SDK Middleware vs Genkit Middleware: A Hands-On Comparison】Vercel AI SDK 中间件与 Genkit 中间件：实际比较
item.description:
【

Two of the most popular Gen AI frameworks in JavaScript/TypeScript, Vercel AI SDK and Genkit, both ship a middleware system to extend their model calls with cross-cutting behavior: logging, caching, RAG, retries, fallbacks, guardrails, tool approval, etc.

On the surface they look very similar. In practice, they sit at different abstraction levels and embody different philosophies. This article puts them side by side using their official docs as the source of truth:

】

JavaScript/TypeScript 中最流行的两个 Gen AI 框架，Vercel AI SDK 和 Genkit，都提供中间件系统，以通过横切行为扩展其模型调用：日志记录、缓存、RAG、重试、回退、护栏、工具批准等。

从表面上看，它们看起来非常相似。在实践中，它们处于不同的抽象层次并体现不同的哲学。本文将它们并列使用其官方文档作为事实来源：

item.title: 【Designing Effective Meetings in Tech: From Time Wasters to Strategic Tools】设计有效的技术会议：从浪费时间到战略工具
item.description:
【

If you’ve been in software engineering long enough — especially as a senior, staff engineer, architect, or tech executive — you’ve felt it: meetings that drain energy, fragment your focus, and somehow still fail to move anything forward.

The irony is almost historical. The word meeting comes from the Old English mētan — “to encounter” or “to come together with purpose.” Yet in modern organizations, especially in IT, meetings often represent the opposite: diffusion instead of direction.

】

如果您在软件工程领域工作了足够长的时间，尤其是作为一名高级工程师、架构师或技术主管，您就会感觉到：会议会耗尽精力、分散您的注意力，而且不知怎么的仍然无法推动任何事情的进展。

这种讽刺几乎是历史性的。 会议一词源自古英语mētan ——“相遇”或“带着目标走到一起”。然而，在现代组织中，尤其是在 IT 组织中，会议往往代表着相反的含义：扩散而不是方向。

item.title: 【Your Identity Governance Is Lying to You】你的身份治理在骗你
item.description:
【

There's a specific kind of compliance theater that anyone who's worked in enterprise security will recognize. It's quarterly access review season. A manager opens their inbox, sees 400 certification tasks due by Friday, and starts clicking "Approve" — not because they've reviewed anything, but because the deadline is real and the access list is incomprehensible. By Friday afternoon, the IGA platform shows 100% completion. The audit passes.

Nothing about that process made the environment more secure. But it generated artifacts that look like governance.

】

有一种特定类型的合规性剧场，任何从事企业安全工作的人都会认识到。现在是季度访问审核季节。一位经理打开收件箱，看到周五到期的 400 项认证任务，然后开始点击“批准”——不是因为他们已经审查了任何内容，而是因为截止日期是真实的，而访问列表难以理解。截至周五下午，IGA 平台已完成 100%。审核通过。

这个过程并没有让环境变得更安全。但它产生了看起来像治理的工件。
